nodes.label: Manager Pattern
nodes.rationale: Core functionality is organized into Audio/Model/Transcription managers initialized at startup and held in Tauri state, keeping business logic cohesive and testable.

nodes.label: Command-Event Architecture
nodes.rationale: Frontend calls Backend via Tauri commands; Backend notifies Frontend via events, giving a clear one-directional-per-channel boundary.

nodes.label: Pipeline Processing
nodes.rationale: Audio flows through VAD to Whisper/Parakeet to text output to clipboard/paste as a linear pipeline.

nodes.label: State Flow
nodes.rationale: State moves Zustand to Tauri Command to Rust State to persistence via tauri-plugin-store.

nodes.label: Single Instance Architecture
nodes.rationale: Launching a second time brings the settings window to front; remote-control flags are delivered by a second instance that forwards args via tauri_plugin_single_instance then exits.

nodes.label: CLI Parameters
nodes.rationale: CLI flags are runtime-only overrides that never modify persisted settings, keeping scripted control separate from saved config.

nodes.label: Internationalization (i18n)
nodes.rationale: All user-facing strings use i18next and ESLint forbids hardcoded JSX strings.

nodes.label: ONNX Runtime
nodes.rationale: Prebuilt ONNX Runtime binaries are unavailable for Intel Macs, so it must be installed via Homebrew and linked dynamically with ORT_LIB_LOCATION/ORT_PREFER_DYNAMIC_LINK.

nodes.label: AppImage linuxdeploy strip issue
nodes.rationale: linuxdeploy bundles an old strip binary that fails on rolling-release distro libraries; the deb/rpm/binary bundles build fine, so the workaround is to skip AppImage with --bundles deb.

nodes.label: CLAUDE.md AGENTS Reference
nodes.rationale: CLAUDE.md simply defers to AGENTS.md as the single source of assistant guidance.

nodes.label: SpeakoFlow Philosophy
nodes.rationale: Prioritizes Simplicity, Extensibility, Privacy, and Accessibility so the project stays a forkable, well-patterned, local-first foundation for the community.

nodes.label: AI Assistance Disclosure
nodes.rationale: AI-assisted PRs are welcomed provided contributors disclose which tools were used and how extensively.

nodes.label: Contribution Development Workflow
nodes.rationale: Feature-branch, test, conventional-commit, rebase-on-upstream, and community-feedback-first workflow keeps the project focused and avoids feature creep.

nodes.label: Translation Contribution Workflow
nodes.rationale: Translators copy the English source, translate only values while preserving keys and {{variables}}, register the language in languages.ts, and never translate brand names.

nodes.label: SpeakoFlow
nodes.rationale: A free, local-first desktop voice assistant bundling dictation, an AI chat panel, screen vision, and spoken answers under one global hotkey.

nodes.label: Handy (upstream project)
nodes.rationale: SpeakoFlow forked Handy by CJ Pais; the local dictation core (Whisper/Parakeet, VAD, overlay, settings) traces back to that MIT-licensed project.

nodes.label: Dictation (local STT)
nodes.rationale: Speech-to-text runs 100% locally on GPU/CPU so audio never leaves the machine.

nodes.label: Assistant Panel
nodes.rationale: A floating always-on-top glass panel streams LLM answers with conversation memory and a collapse-to-pill mode.

nodes.label: Screen Vision
nodes.rationale: Captures the active monitor and sends it to a vision-capable model; captures are adaptively compressed to fit strict provider payload limits.

nodes.label: Web Search
nodes.rationale: Optional, opt-in, snippet-first search across five backends so the assistant can answer current factual questions instead of guessing from stale training data.

nodes.label: Spoken Answers (TTS)
nodes.rationale: Assistant replies can be read aloud via Kokoro (local), OpenAI-compatible, ElevenLabs, or Azure engines with Markdown stripped first.

nodes.label: Privacy Model
nodes.rationale: Voice-to-text is always local; assistant questions go only to the provider the user configures; search sends only the query; TTS is local by default. No telemetry, accounts, or cloud middleman.

nodes.label: Built-in Local LLM
nodes.rationale: A bundled offline LLM (llama.cpp) lets the assistant run fully offline with no server to set up.

nodes.label: Code Style Guidelines
nodes.rationale: Rust uses anyhow errors and Arc<Mutex<T>> shared state; TypeScript/React uses functional components, Zod schemas, and grouped type imports.

nodes.label: ElevenLabs Design System
nodes.rationale: An editorial, print-magazine visual language: off-white canvas, warm near-black ink, no saturated CTA color, brand voltage carried by photography and modest type weights.

nodes.label: Waldenburg Light Typography
nodes.rationale: Licensed display serif kept at weight 300 as the editorial signature; EB Garamond or GT Sectra are documented open-source substitutes.

nodes.label: Inter Font
nodes.rationale: Carries body, navigation, and captions at 400/500 with slightly looser +0.15-0.18px tracking for an editorial feel.

nodes.label: Atmospheric Gradient Orbs
nodes.rationale: Five pastel radial orbs (mint/peach/lavender/sky/rose) act purely as atmospheric decoration, never as button fills or text colors.

nodes.label: Editorial Magazine Aesthetic
nodes.rationale: The brand trusts atmospheric photography and modest type weights instead of a saturated accent so it reads like a quiet editorial print magazine rather than a dev-tools app.

nodes.label: Deterministic Text Replacements
nodes.rationale: A rule-based literal/regex find-replace pass plus magic commands, isolated in process_transcription_output; the [run] shell command is gated behind an off-by-default opt-in for safety.

nodes.label: Secure API-Key Storage (planned)
nodes.rationale: Move provider secrets from plaintext settings_store.json into the OS keychain via keyring, hydrating an in-memory cache once so the get_settings hot path stays fast.

nodes.label: Eager/Streaming Transcription
nodes.rationale: Transcribe segments while recording for the biggest perceived-latency win; it rewrites the shared recorder/transcription/coordinator core so it must ship last and alone.

nodes.label: Product Purpose
nodes.rationale: A free local-first voice assistant that replaces paid Wispr Flow-style subscriptions; success is the user trusting it as their default voice layer.

nodes.label: Brand Personality
nodes.rationale: Calm, premium, quiet, and trustworthy: a confident editorial instrument, not a noisy dashboard.

nodes.label: Design Principles
nodes.rationale: Quiet by default, hierarchy over uniformity, trust through legibility, stay out of the way, and one deliberate color moment carry the premium feel through craft rather than ornament.

nodes.label: Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA)
nodes.rationale: Targets WCAG 2.1 AA contrast, full keyboard operability with visible focus, prefers-reduced-motion support, RTL/i18n as first-class, and never signals state by color alone.

nodes.label: Anti-references
nodes.rationale: Explicitly avoids generic Tauri settings UIs, busy SaaS dashboards, equal-weight control panels, neon gamer aesthetics, and AI-slop tells to protect the quiet-premium identity.

nodes.label: Assistant Mode (Phase 1)
nodes.rationale: Added record-to-LLM streaming into a frameless glass panel with an SSE client and a cache-friendly append-only request design to maximize provider prompt-cache hits.

nodes.label: Azure Payload Saga
nodes.rationale: Three rounds of Azure 400 errors on image_url forced an aggressive compression ladder targeting <=48 KB encoded, HTTP/1.1, and capped history, since vision models downscale internally so lost resolution costs little.

nodes.label: Secure API-Key Storage (implemented)
nodes.rationale: secret_store.rs wraps keyring v3 with a process-wide cache, one-time plaintext migration, per-target native backends, a dedicated worker thread, and graceful no-op fallback when no store is available.

nodes.label: Web Search Evolution
nodes.rationale: Evolved from a snippet-only speed-first design to an LLM search planner, then to a reliability/quality overhaul that dropped Firecrawl full-page fetch and settled on five snippet-first SERP providers with deterministic time-sensitivity overrides.

nodes.label: LLM Providers
nodes.rationale: All LLM providers share one OpenAI-style /chat/completions code path with headers built in build_headers(), so new OpenAI-compatible providers need no new request logic.

nodes.label: Web Search Providers
nodes.rationale: Serper, Brave, Tavily, Exa, and SerpAPI are all snippet-first single-round-trip providers whose freshness token maps to each API's own parameter.

nodes.label: TTS Engines
nodes.rationale: Kokoro (local), OpenAI-compatible /audio/speech, ElevenLabs, and Azure AI Speech were each diffed against current official docs.

nodes.label: Serper (search)
nodes.rationale: Default web-search provider; POST to google.serper.dev/search with an X-API-KEY header, retried once without the time filter because it intermittently returns an empty 200.

nodes.label: Kokoro (local TTS)
nodes.rationale: Runs fully in the webview via kokoro-js (WebGPU/wasm), model onnx-community/Kokoro-82M-v1.0-ONNX, keyless and never reaching Rust.

nodes.label: ElevenLabs TTS
nodes.rationale: POST to elevenlabs.io text-to-speech with an xi-api-key header; mp3_44100_64 is a valid free-tier format and eleven_flash_v2_5 the default model.

nodes.label: Azure OpenAI
nodes.rationale: Works only against the v1 endpoint (/openai/v1); the client now sends the api-key header alongside Bearer and normalizes any pasted Azure host, but the classic dated deployment endpoint is out of scope.

nodes.label: Prompts Reference (map of every LLM instruction)
nodes.norm_label: prompts reference (map of every llm instruction)

nodes.label: P2 — Logo & Icon System
nodes.rationale: Icons kept white on transparent for the dark Windows taskbar/tray/installer target; a deliberate tradeoff that is faint on light chrome.

nodes.label: P3 — Update System, Tray Home & About
nodes.rationale: Renamed tray Settings->Home to avoid two items opening the same window; reused the proven show_main_window path.
nodes.norm_label: p3 — update system, tray home & about

nodes.label: P5 — History Limit & Recording Retention Fix
nodes.norm_label: p5 — history limit & recording retention fix

nodes.label: P6 — Provider Audit (LLM, Web Search, TTS)
nodes.norm_label: p6 — provider audit (llm, web search, tts)

nodes.label: P14 — Security Review & Pre-release QA Gate
nodes.community_name: P14 — Security Review & Pre-release QA G
nodes.norm_label: p14 — security review & pre-release qa gate

nodes.label: Snippet-first retrieval
nodes.rationale: Retrieval quality and reranking, not the LLM, was the bottleneck; the snippet/answer box usually answers the question, so full-page scraping was dropped to cut latency and noise.

nodes.label: DuckDuckGo removal
nodes.rationale: Keyless DuckDuckGo HTML scraping was brittle, low quality and often returned nothing, degrading turns to stale training-data answers; once Serper became the default it added no value and was removed.
